pages.raw_text:
Members of the street car mems
union say that Starter Sweeney
was largely responsible for the dis
ruption of the union the men for
merly had in this city. They state
Sweeney reported the proceedings
of tn union tc the officers of the
Washington Water Power company
with the result that the company
knew who was active in the union
such men being discharged as soon
as possible. Just before the old
union went out of business
Sweeney was expelled, but the
harm was done and the union de-
stroyed. Men employed on the
Traction Inns Gle i'm that Sweeney
is at his old game of spying around
for the Washington Water Power
company. They claim that he has
attempted to extract information
from members of the Garments
union employed by the Traction
company as to who belongs to the
union and are employed by the
Washington Water Power COM
pany.. They claim Sweeney owes
his present affluence and position
with the company to his contempt
ible actions with regard TO the men
with whom he has been employed
and with whom he should have
common interests.
